name: Assemblies Of God
description_short: NCUA-insured credit union based in Springfield, MO, offering savings accounts, CDs, and loans to Assembly of God members and affiliated organizations since 1951.
description_long:
Assemblies Of God Credit Union was established in 1951 and operates as a member-focused financial institution with approximately $432.27 million in assets. The credit union is NCUA-insured and state-chartered (Charter #62897), headquartered at 1535 N Campbell Ave in Springfield, Missouri. As a specialized credit union, it serves a defined membership base rather than the general public.

The institution offers a range of deposit products including savings accounts, money market accounts, and certificates of deposit (CDs) with terms ranging from 6 months to 5 years. Current deposit rates include 0.10% APY on savings accounts (minimum $2,500) and CD rates ranging from 0.35% APY on 6-month CDs to 1.41% APY on 5-year CDs. The credit union also provides loan products, though specific loan rates were not publicly available at the time of data collection. Current loans outstanding total $335.54 million against $356.43 million in member deposits.

Assemblies Of God Credit Union distinguishes itself through its membership restrictions and mission-aligned focus. Membership is limited to direct and relevant associates, ministers, missionaries, employees, or students of the Assembly of God Ministry, their family members, and board-approved affiliated organizations. This specialized field of membership creates a community-focused institution rather than a general-purpose credit union. The institution maintains conservative capital levels ($38.65 million) relative to its asset base.

For prospective members, the primary limitation is membership eligibility—this credit union is not open to the general public. Additionally, deposit rates are notably below national average rates for savings and CDs as of April 2026, making it less competitive on yield compared to online alternatives. Loan rate information was unavailable during the data collection period, suggesting limited transparency in this area. Many banks offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.
services: Money market accounts with tiered rates up to 0.25% APY
Personal and home loans (rates not disclosed)
Home equity lines of credit (HELOC) products
pros: NCUA insurance protection on all deposits up to $250,000
Established institution with 75+ years of operational history (founded 1951)
Offers full range of deposit products: savings, money market, and CDs from 6 months to 5 years
Membership-based model provides community focus and alignment with Assembly of God values
Multiple CD term options (6, 12, 18, 24, 36, 48, 60 months) with consistent rates across deposit tiers
Capital-to-asset ratio of 8.95% demonstrates financial stability
cons: Restricted membership eligibility excludes the general public—only for Assembly of God affiliates, ministers, employees, and their families
Deposit rates significantly below national averages (0.10% savings vs. 4.20% market rate; 1.41% 5-year CD vs. 4.00% market rate)
Loan rates not publicly available; requires direct contact with credit union for rate inquiries
No information provided on loan products, terms, or availability despite being a stated financial service
Limited branch network information provided; membership geographic coverage unclear
best_for: Assembly of God ministers, missionaries, and employees seeking faith-aligned banking
Members of affiliated AG organizations and their families needing NCUA-insured accounts
Conservative savers prioritizing institution stability and insurance protection over competitive yields
Individuals within the AG membership community seeking relationship-based banking services
diagnosis: Assemblies Of God Credit Union is exclusively for members of the Assembly of God ministry community and affiliated organizations—not available to general consumers. Even for eligible members, deposit rates lag significantly behind market averages, making it a choice based on mission alignment and institutional stability rather than yield optimization.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
